title: Allow configuring the gpg program used for signing & verification
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

I would like to be able to sign containers using podman, but using a specific gpg binary to do the actual signing.  Something similar to git's `gpg.program` config.  (More specifically, I would like to use Qubes OS [Split GPG setup](https://www.qubes-os.org/doc/split-gpg/).)

I dug around a bit and I believe this can be done by allowing the user to overwrite the second argument of `SetEngineInfo` in [github.com/containers/image/signature](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/2bb3f3e44c5cbe532a3cdbe735f78c21418f8dc2/signature/mechanism_gpgme.go#L81).  I should probably open an issue on containers/image, but I wanted to check first that it would be ok to expose this through podman somehow (option, config, env var…)

comments.body: I would have no problem with it.

I’m fine with that in principle. 

Just noting that it would be a fairly extensive change in c/image, most of the policy code is not currently carrying around a `types.SystemContext`. (And at this point I’m not sure if we want to do that, or design and start using some very different option mechanism.)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I could give it a go, but most likely not before one or two weeks.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@gregoire-mullvad Did you ever get a chance?

@mtrmac @vrothberg Would it make more sense to define the gpg program inside of registries.conf rather then allowing users on the fly to choose one.  I don't think this will be variable per container, more of a system wide setting.

`registries.conf` is clearly not the right file. Not that we _have_ the right file…
- `policy.json` is conceptually close, but “policy” is not a great fit for implementation details like this (especially when c/image can be built so that it does not use GPG at all; would it ignore that setting, or fail?)
- `containers.conf` is general enough, but not used by Skopeo. Still, I guess it’s the closest match.

(Actually building a compatible API extension throughout c/image to allow passing that configuration would be a the bigger part of the work.)

I did start looking into this a bit. My initial ideas was to duplicate [`NewGPGSigningMechanism()`](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/2bb3f3e44c5cbe532a3cdbe735f78c21418f8dc2/signature/mechanism.go#L59) into something like `NewGPGSigningMechanismWithProgram(gpgProgram string)`. @mtrmac do you think that would be an acceptable way to pass this configuration to c/image?
(I considered something like `NewGPGSigningMechanismWithOpts` which would take a struct that can later be extended, but it seems that there aren't a lot more options can be passed to gpgme, and if the user can override the gpg binary they can write a wrapper that sets whatever option they want anyway.)

As for the user interface, I was thinking of just adding a new `--gpg-program` flag to `podman-image-sign` to start with.

> I did start looking into this a bit. My initial ideas was to duplicate [`NewGPGSigningMechanism()`](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/2bb3f3e44c5cbe532a3cdbe735f78c21418f8dc2/signature/mechanism.go#L59) into something like `NewGPGSigningMechanismWithProgram(gpgProgram string)`.

We would also want that for `newEphemeralGPGSigningMechanism` for consistency; and then the value needs to propagate through `prSignedBy.isSignatureAuthorAccepted`, i.e. probably be somehow configurable when creating a `PolicyContext`. So that, and the `copy.copier.createSignature` path, both needs to pass through GPG options without really caring what they are — and that suggests to me that we do need a struct, not just so that it can be extended, but also so that the large call stack doesn’t need to manage items individually.

And the generic `PolicyContext`, which isn’t really GPG-exclusive, should probably define the configuration mechanism of the new variant of `NewPolicyContext` in a way that can be extended for other implementation choices, so not just a `NewPolicyContextWithGPGOptions`.

---

(I could see an argument for adding the option only to `NewGPGSigningMechanism`, not the ephemeral one, on the theory that for signing it’s more important to have a choice of how to manage the private keys, while the ephemeral verifiers don’t strictly need to allow users’ implementation choice. I don’t quite agree but I don’t feel strongly about it.)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I thought a bit more about this, and I think it's not obvious that the ephemeral mechanism should be changed for consistency. At least in the use case I described above, gpg-split in Qubes, the "alternative" gpg program will ignore `GNUPGHOME` by design.

So it might be dangerous to try to set `GNUPGHOME` while explicitly allowing the user to chose the gpg program. And in that case, maybe it makes sense to only allow changing the program in the non-ephemeral case and only add the configuration option for signing, and not for verification?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Conversation on this seems to have died out, I just saw it in the stale-issues list.
@gregoire-mullvad is this something you are still interested in working on?
Yes. I can try to open a PR on containers/image this or next week.
